title: Electing Members to the Joint Committee of Congress on the Library and the Joint Committee on Printing.
plain_summary: This bill picks certain members of the House of Representatives to serve on two special committees that work with both the House and Senate. One committee is in charge of running the Library of Congress, which is the big library that serves all of Congress. The other committee handles all the printing that the government does, like making copies of bills and reports. These committees have members from both the House and Senate working together. The bill simply names which House members will join these committees.
last_action_text: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

sections.title: Section 1. Election of Members to Joint Committee of Congress on the Library and Joint Committee on Printing
sections.text: This section chooses members of Congress to serve on two special committees. The first committee is called the Joint Committee of Congress on the Library. This committee works with libraries and library matters. Four people will serve on this committee. They are Mr. Carey, Mr. Morelle, and Ms. Johnson of Texas. These three will work alongside the person who leads the Committee on House Administration and the person who leads the Subcommittee on the Legislative Branch of the Committee on Appropriations. The second committee is called the Joint Committee on Printing. This committee deals with government printing matters. Four people will serve on this committee. They are Mr. Morelle, Mr. Murphy, Ms. Sewell, and Mrs. Miller of Illinois. These four will work alongside the person who leads the Committee on House Administration.
